text: Figures 20.7 and 20.8 connected minima and critical points (../../figures/page_0410_fig_2.jpg)

Figure 20.7 and Figure 20.8 Loss-surface structure. Figure 20.7 shows that for sufficiently deep and wide networks, a curved low-energy path can connect two independently discovered minima. Figure 20.8 shows that, in random Gaussian functions and neural network loss surfaces, lower-loss critical points tend to have fewer downward-curving directions.

Figure 20.9 Goldilocks zone. The proportion of Hessian eigenvalues that are greater than zero in a random subspace of dimension $D\_s$ is plotted against squared parameter radius relative to Xavier initialization; a pronounced region of positive curvature is known as the Goldilocks zone. Adapted from Fort & Scherlis (2019).
